key_points: Erbium lasers (Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) demonstrate effective and selective caries ablation, smear layer removal, and improved adhesion in restorative dentistry.

Laser-activated irrigation protocols such as photon-induced photoacoustic streaming (PIPS) and shock wave-enhanced emission photoacoustic streaming (SWEEPS) enhance irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption in endodontics.
In prosthodontics and orthodontics, erbium lasers allow minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets while preserving underlying structures.
In periodontology and peri-implantitis, lasers improve debridement, support regeneration, and enhance wound healing.
Photobiomodulation (PBM) contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair.
Outcomes vary with wavelength, energy settings, and clinical protocols, underscoring the need for standardization.
The review is aligned with the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers to support globally consistent clinical practice.
Further high-quality randomized controlled trials are necessary to define standardized laser protocols across dental specialties.

outcomes.result: Erbium lasers demonstrate effective and selective caries ablation and smear layer removal
outcomes.clinical_significance: Improved adhesion and minimally invasive caries management

outcomes.measured: Endodontic irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption
outcomes.result: PIPS and SWEEPS protocols enhance irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption

outcomes.measured: Debonding of prosthetic and orthodontic elements
outcomes.result: Erbium lasers allow minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets
Lasers improve debridement, support regeneration, and enhance wound healing
outcomes.clinical_significance: Adjunctive benefit in periodontal and peri-implant therapy

outcomes.measured: Pain reduction and tissue repair via PBM
outcomes.result: Photobiomodulation contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair
outcomes.clinical_significance: Enhanced patient comfort and biological healing

notes: Narrative review linked to the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers. No specific laser parameters are reported as this is an overview review. Covers multiple dental specialties: caries management, endodontics, prosthodontics, orthodontics, periodontology, and peri-implant therapy.
source_citation: Grzech-Leśniak, K. (2026). Lasers in Dentistry - Overview Based on FDI Policy Statement. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2025.109369
clinical_indication: Multiple dental applications: caries removal, endodontic disinfection, periodontal therapy, peri-implant therapy, prosthetic and orthodontic debonding, photobiomodulation for pain and tissue repair
target_tissue: dentin, enamel, pulp, gingiva, bone, implant surface
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (wavelength settings, power, fluence, pulse duration) are reported in the abstract; full text may contain more detail but was not available.
No conflict of interest or funding information available in the provided metadata.
As a narrative review, the evidence synthesis may be subject to selection bias by the author.
